Resonance parameters data and cross sections plots.
Short description of the
experiment.Resonance parameters data and cross
section plots.
Short descript. of the experiment.
Preliminary experiment,
data for 56,58Fe, 58,60Ni
-A measurement of the total neutron cross section
of iron-58 in the energy range from 7 to 325 keV.-
Pulsed 3MeV Van-de-Graaff accelerator at
Karlsruhe.
Neutrons were generated via the 7-Li(p,n)
reaction in thick Li-targets.
.Samples consisted of powdered oxide (Fe(2)-O(3),
65.09% enriched to Fe-58) pressed into thin
1.1-cm-diam aluminium containers. For the transmis-
sion experiments 2.4157 and 7.15 grams, for the
capture experiments 16.35 grams of the above iron
oxide have been used.
Time of flight technique. Energy resolution
0.6 ns/m, flight path 499.1 cm for total cross
sections measurements.Energy resolution 2 ns/m,
flight path 1.93 m for capture measurements.
Li-6 glass scintillator (10.5 cm dia. x 1.3 cm
thickness, 6.6% Li enriched in Li-6 to 95%.
The capture events were detected by means of
their gamma-rays with an 800 l liquid scintillation
tank.
.The transmission data were analyzed by the shape
fit code FANAL II which used an R- matrix formalism
as described by Lane and Thomas in Rev.Mod. Phys.
30(1958)257. For the resonance radiation widths
the Monte Carlo code TACASI as described by Froehner
in General Atomic Report GA-6906(1966) has been used.
.For multiple scattering and resonance self shielding
Statistical errors.

.For astrophysical applications a Maxwellian averaged
capture cross section at kT = 30 keV of 24.6+-6.5 mb
for Fe-58 was calculated.

given in report: INDC(GER)-20,18,1977: 24.+-6. mb.
